stories.title: SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
stories.simple_summary: A coding tool made by SpaceXAI was secretly sending all of users' computer code to Google's cloud storage without permission, even code the users told it to ignore. The company turned it off after this was discovered.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use this tool for your small business's software, your private code could have been exposed. However, this is mostly fixed now, so you only need to worry if you were using this specific tool recently.

stories.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
stories.simple_summary: Google is being sued by major book publishers who say Google trained its AI system on their copyrighted books without asking permission first.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a legal fight between big companies — it doesn't directly affect most small business owners or regular people right now.

stories.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
stories.simple_summary: A leader at Google's AI lab is suggesting that AI companies need an independent group (like a referee) to check that new powerful AI systems are safe and fair before releasing them.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech industry professionals and investors — for you, it just means there might be better safety checks on AI tools in the future.

stories.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
stories.simple_summary: Former employees at Meta are suing the company, saying Meta used AI tools to unfairly decide which workers to lay off, targeting people who were already on leave.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run a small business and use AI to make hiring or firing decisions, this is a warning that you need to be very careful to treat people fairly and not let AI introduce hidden bias.

stories.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
stories.simple_summary: Meta's Instagram leader thinks companies will soon need to limit how much each engineer can spend on using AI tools, similar to how they limit budgets for other expenses.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about how tech companies manage their own spending internally — unless you're a tech company yourself, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
stories.simple_summary: Google Images is redesigning its homepage to show you pictures it thinks you might like, similar to how Pinterest works, instead of just showing a blank search page.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google Images, you'll see recommended pictures before you search, which might help you discover things but also means Google is tracking what you like.

stories.title: The Google Images homepage will recommend photos even before you search
stories.simple_summary: Google Images is changing its homepage to show you recommended pictures based on your interests before you even search for anything.
stories.why_it_matters: This is the same change as the previous story — Google Images will recommend pictures to you automatically now.

stories.title: Spotify is now an AI chatbot, too
stories.simple_summary: Spotify is testing a new feature where you can type or talk to the app to ask it what music or podcasts to play, like having a conversation with someone who knows your taste.
stories.why_it_matters: If you pay for Spotify Premium, you'll soon be able to find music by just describing what you want instead of searching — it's more convenient, but Spotify will learn even more about what you like.

stories.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
stories.simple_summary: New York State is pausing approval for new large computer data centers (buildings that store data and power AI) because they use a lot of electricity and water.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live in New York, this means AI companies can't easily build new facilities there, which might slow down local AI development but could help keep your electric bills lower.

stories.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
stories.simple_summary: A new AI company made a $1 billion deal to rent computer power from another company so it can train and run its AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech investors and engineers — it's just a business deal between two companies working on AI behind the scenes.

stories.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
stories.simple_summary: A leader at an AI research company says that most real-world businesses want to use simpler, more affordable AI models instead of the newest, most powerful AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: For small business owners, this is good news — it suggests practical AI tools will get cheaper and more available instead of only the biggest companies having access.

stories.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
stories.simple_summary: Spotify is launching an AI chat feature for Premium users that lets you have a conversation to find and play music, podcasts, and audiobooks.
stories.why_it_matters: If you pay for Spotify, you can soon just chat with the app to ask for music instead of typing searches — it's more natural and convenient.
